entries.title: Call with John Tabor — March 2026
entries.body:
**Context:** Tabor called after reading Housing Authority board application.

**Key takeaways:**
- Committee tasked with turning advocate framework into executable action plan
- Housing is complex — combination of removing zoning barriers, putting more land into use, increasing density, financial tools
- Getting a crash course in land use regulation is a major area of progress
- Always looking at tax abatements, tax deferrals as financial levers
- Mentioned Nashua as a city to study for enabling legislation
- Zoning rewrite is a major 2027 goal

**My questions to follow up on:**
- Which zoning provisions are the biggest bottlenecks right now?
- Has the GNOD generated developer interest since it passed?
- Is the Housing Trust Fund being actively deployed?
- Where would a data/analytics lens be most useful?

entries.title: Key Takeaways — Gerry Duffy / Ollie Chag Loaded Question Progress Portsmouth Meeting (3/20/26)
entries.body:
Who is Gerry Duffy

	∙	Founder of Progress Portsmouth, been at it ~2 years
	∙	Not elected, not an insider — positioned as an outside advocate/watchdog, “be the teeth”
	∙	Has same frustrations with Portsmouth Listens process that others have expressed
	∙	Uses multiple AIs for his own research
	∙	Maintains a public GitHub repo with the 137-item Housing Action Plan framework — this is the spine of everything

The 137 Items

The central concept from the meeting. Progress Portsmouth built a 137-item framework for the Portsmouth Housing Action Plan (due July 2026):

	∙	Council voted 9-0 Feb 17 to use this framework
	∙	PHC will triage and prioritize the 137 items
	∙	PHC can push items to Planning Board or Planning Dept. depending on type
	∙	Goal: keep council accountable to actually implementing the plan
	∙	Right now the framework lives in Gerry’s GitHub — needs to move to something more official

How the PHC Works

	∙	PHC is advisory — sends recommendations up to council every 2 weeks
	∙	Should be collaborative with Planning Dept. (staff) and Planning Board
	∙	Planning Dept. is capacity-constrained — PHC needs to be strategic about what it asks of them
	∙	Planning Board improved significantly in the last year — Logan Roy and others now on it. Andrew Samonas potential conflict of interest*
	∙	Brian Chelner = Chair of Planning Board, fan of co-living
	∙	Jim Hewitt was kicked off Planning Board — cautionary tale on conflicts of interest

Key People to Know / Meet

	∙	Gerry Duffy — Progress Portsmouth, key outside ally
	∙	Jo Kelley + John Tabor — co-chairs of Housing Committee, direct oversight — meet for coffee ASAP, convey urgency
	∙	Brian Chelner — Planning Board chair, pro-housing, fan of co-living
	∙	Byron Matteo — housing employee, also on school board
	∙	Rebecca Perkins Kwoka — NH State Senate, Portsmouth, housing ally — Tabor trying to get her engaged
	∙	John O’Leary — involved in housing, worth clarifying role, been an issue at meetings
	∙	Dagan — (Liars) on committee
